meaning: n.<美口> 元,雄鹿,公羊,公兔v.(马等)突然一跃(将骑手摔下)
examples: We stayed at Tom's place. It isn't exactly Buck House, but it's comfortable enough.
Buck never allowed himself to be bullied into doing anything that went against his principles.
In the afternoon, they go back out, hoping for that buck.
But the Churchill Arms in Kensington was one of the very first to buck the trend.
Monks has come up with a hardball tactic to pressure companies--while making a buck or two.
